seo.metaDescription: 9-hour small-group eastern Jeju loop: Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO), Micheon lava cave, Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO), Haenyeo Museum. 4.9/5.
seo.title: Jeju East UNESCO Day Tour · All Admissions · 4.9/5 | AtoC
seo.description: East Jeju UNESCO day tour: Seongsan Ilchulbong → Seongeup Folk Village → Haenyeo Museum → Micheon Cave lava tube → Hamdeok Beach. All admissions included, small group, certified guide. 4.9/5 (1,148 reviews).

catalog_card.title: Jeju East UNESCO Day Tour
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A small-group day tour covering eastern Jeju's UNESCO and signature stops in a single 9-hour loop — Hamdeok Seoubong Beach, Seongeup Folk Village, the Micheon Cave lava tube system (current alternate to closed Manjanggul), Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO Sunrise Peak), and the Haenyeo Museum dedicated to Jeju's UNESCO-listed women divers. ALL admissions included; certified English/Chinese-speaking guide; rated 4.9/5 across 1,148 reviews.
catalog_card.subtitle: Small-group mini-coach with English/Chinese guide — 9-hour eastern Jeju loop covering Hamdeok, Seongeup, Micheon Cave, Seongsan UNESCO crater, and the Haenyeo Museum.

itineraryStops.name: Pickup (4 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description:
**Pickup is consolidated at four central Jeju City points** to minimize morning crisscrossing: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for guests with same-day arrivals or hotels near the airport corridor; **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for guests in the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster; **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** as the westernmost pickup before heading east; and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** as the optional eastern Jeju City pickup near Tapdong waterfront. 

Sequence is Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → eastward Route 1132 toward Hamdeok. The minicoach is **small-group (max ~14 pax)** with English-speaking guide. 

**Bilingual printed itinerary card** distributed at pickup, weather brief at first stop.
highlights: **4 pickup points** across Jeju City — Airport, City Hall, Hagwi, Dongmun
Sequence west→east minimizes morning coach distance
**Small-group minicoach (max 14 pax)** with English guide
Bilingual itinerary card + weather brief at first stop
Total morning transit Jeju City → Hamdeok: ~40 min
Drop-off mirrors pickup with optional Dongmun Market end-of-day stop
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Four pickup points cover Jeju City's main hotel + transport zones (airport, mid-city, old town, market district) without forcing guests across the city. Sequencing west→east lands the coach at Hamdeok ~09:50 — early enough to catch the morning low-tide beach panorama before peak visitor flow.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.category: Top-3 Jeju Beach — Olle Trail 19 + WWII Coastal History
itineraryStops.description:
**Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (함덕 서우봉 해변)** is one of Jeju's best-known east-coast beaches, loved for its bright sand, shallow emerald water, and the small volcanic cone of **Seoubong (서우봉)** rising at the eastern end. 

The main beach and connected coves give guests an easy first look at Jeju's coastal scenery without a long walk, and the seaside promenade is suitable for families and seniors. 

In spring, the slopes around Seoubong often turn yellow with canola flowers; in clear weather, the short upper path gives a broad view over the eastern coastline. 

The area also has a historical layer: coastal caves and wartime remains around Seoubong are connected with Japan's late-colonial military presence on Jeju, adding context beyond the beach view. Hamdeok is also part of **Jeju Olle Trail Course 19**, so guests can experience a small piece of Jeju's walking culture before moving inland. 

Admission is free, and the stop works well as a relaxed photo and orientation point at the beginning of the eastern route.
highlights: **Top 3 Jeju beaches** — emerald-turquoise water + white coral sand
**500 m main beach** + 3 connected coves (~900 m total length)
**Seoubong volcanic cone** ~1-hour summit climb — canola-yellow slopes March–April
**WWII Imperial Japanese Army tunnels** in adjacent cliffs — kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Olle Trail Course 19 (조천 → 김녕)** — blue-orange ribbon trail markers
**Cafe Delmoondo** — large terraces, beachfront, signature stop
Up to **50,000 daily summer visitors** at peak; uncrowded spring + autumn
**FREE admission**; 20 km / ~40 min from Jeju International Airport
Mediterranean-like color contrast — rare on the Korean Peninsula
timeUsed: Cafe Delmoondo or beachfront photography — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Coastal opening highlight of the eastern UNESCO route.** Hamdeok gives the day an easy start with Jeju's beach scenery, a short coastal walk, and a light historical layer before the route turns inland toward Seongeup. It is naturally placed after Jeju City on the eastbound road, so guests get a meaningful first stop without unnecessary backtracking. Compared with beaches farther east or inside Jeju City, Hamdeok balances scenery, access, walking comfort, and route efficiency especially well for a one-day tour.

sources: Jeju Olle Foundation — Trail Course 19 official map
VisitJeju — Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
Jeju 4·3 Peace Foundation — Imperial Japanese Army tunnel records
Korea Tourism Organization — Top 3 Jeju Beaches ranking
Jocheon-eup local cultural office — Seoubong oreum trail records

smartNotes.photo: Sunset with Seoubong silhouette is the classic shot; aerial-style shots from Seoubong summit show the white sand curve
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along the beachfront; convenience stores nearby
smartNotes.tip: Less crowded than Hyeopjae or Gimnyeong beaches; the Delmoondo café area on the east side is popular for sunset

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms and shower facilities at beach

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours (beach); managed swim season July–August with lifeguards
visitBasics.walking: Easy — beach is flat sand; Seoubong hike is moderate (steps)

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO heritage)
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. 

Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju architecture, and local stories about daily life in a windy volcanic island environment. 

For visitors, this stop gives a grounded cultural contrast after Hamdeok's coast: guests see how Jeju people historically built homes, protected villages with stone walls, and adapted to a harsh island climate.
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions
timeUsed: Parking + welcome brief by village docent — ~10 min
Walk to ancient trees + village center — ~10 min
Hanok interior visits (2–3 representative homes) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Mid-morning cultural heritage highlight.** Seongeup connects the natural side of eastern Jeju with the island's human history. The visit gives guests a clear look at traditional houses, stone walls, village trees, and old administrative history before the route continues toward the lava-cave and Seongsan area. The stop is long enough for an alley walk, photos, and basic cultural explanation without making the day feel rushed.

sources: Cultural Heritage Administration — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage designation
Korea Cultural Heritage Foundation — Seongeup Folk Village historical records
VisitJeju — Seongeup Minsok Maeul
MBC Drama Archive — Dae Jang Geum (2003–2004) filming locations
Jeju Folklore Society — Omegisul brewing tradition documentation

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

itineraryStops.name: Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land (Manjanggul alternate)
itineraryStops.category: Lava Tube + Theme Park — Manjanggul Alternate (UNESCO-property adjacent)
itineraryStops.description:
**Ilchul Land (일출랜드)** is a large nature theme park in Seongsan built around **Micheon Cave (미천굴)**, a lava-tube cave with a visitor walkway open to the public. It is not Manjanggul Cave, but it offers a practical lava-cave experience when Manjanggul is unavailable or when guests need an easier walking surface. 

Micheon Cave's maintained path, lighting, and controlled route make it more accessible for families, older guests, and visitors who may not be comfortable with rougher cave floors. Outside the cave, Ilchul Land includes gardens, traditional-style Jeju displays, sculptures, and rest areas, so the stop functions as both a geology stop and a weather-friendly break in the middle of the route. 

The cave is cooler than the outside temperature, so a light jacket can be useful even in warmer seasons.
highlights: **1,700 m total** lava tube; **365 m open section** (~25-min walk-through)
**Manjanggul UNESCO alternate** — same lava-tube system; opens when Manjanggul closes
**Floor artificially leveled** — accessible to elderly, strollers, mobility-limited guests
**Seven-color decorative lighting** — gold/silver/bronze/red/black/blue/white
**400,000 m² park grounds** — bonsai, cactus, sculpture, traditional village, footbath cafe
**Only Jeju attraction with on-site Muslim prayer room** — deliberate accessibility
**'Running Man' Ep. 89** filming (2012, Alice-in-Wonderland theme)
Admission **₩12,000 adult / ₩9,000 youth / ₩7,000 child**
**30% indoor / 70% outdoor** — weather-flexible mix
timeUsed: Micheon Cave walk-through (365 m / ~25 min) — ~30 min
Optional bonsai/cactus garden + footbath cafe — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Pre-lunch lava-cave highlight.** This stop gives guests a safe and easy way to understand Jeju's volcanic underground landscape before visiting Seongsan Ilchulbong in the afternoon. It also adds an indoor or semi-indoor portion to a route that otherwise includes many outdoor places. The placement before lunch keeps travel time efficient between Seongeup, Ilchul Land, and the Seongsan area.

sources: Ilchul Land official site (ilchulland.com)
VisitJeju — Ilchul Land Micheon Cave
Korea Cave Research Institute — Manjanggul lava-tube system documentation
SBS Variety Archive — Running Man Episode 89 (2012)
Jeju Tourism Organization — accessibility-friendly attraction registry

smartNotes.facilities: Ilchul Land park amenities (cafe, gift shop, restrooms)
smartNotes.tip: This is the standard Manjanggul alternate when the public Manjanggul Lava Tube is closed for safety inspection. Confirm Ilchul Land cave-section access is included in your group ticket.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (Jeju local restaurant)
itineraryStops.category: Midday Reset — East Jeju Local Cuisine
itineraryStops.description:
**60-minute reset between Ilchulland and Seongsan Ilchulbong** — Seongsan-eup harbor-side restaurant strip catches daily seafood landings. Default rotation: **mulhoe (물회, cold raw-fish soup)** in summer (June–September), **galchi-jorim (갈치조림, braised cutlassfish)** in winter (October–February), or **heuk-doeji gui (흑돼지구이, Jeju black pig BBQ)** year-round. 

Black pig is a heritage breed designated **Cultural Heritage Material No. 5 (2015)**. **Vegetarian + halal-friendly options** available at one venue in the rotation — disclose at pickup. Lunch venue is **10-min coach to Seongsan Ilchulbong** entrance — minimal post-lunch transit before the afternoon climb.
highlights: **East Jeju seafood corridor** — Seongsan-eup harbor-side strip
**Mulhoe (물회)** in summer; **galchi-jorim** winter rotation
**Heuk-doeji** year-round — Cultural Heritage Material No. 5 (2015)
**Vegetarian + halal-friendly** options on request
**10-min coach to Seongsan Ilchulbong** entrance
Group dietary requirements collected at pickup
timeUsed: Coach to Seongsan Ilchulbong entrance — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Seongsan-eup placement = 10-min hop to the cone climb. Mulhoe is an east-Jeju regional specialty (less common in Jeju City), so the geographic placement adds culinary identity to the lunch slot. Pace also breaks the morning's beach + village + cave walking before the afternoon Seongsan ascent + Haenyeo Museum interior.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO Sunrise Peak)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. 

The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. 

**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. 

**Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run **daily at 13:30 and 15:00** at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. 

Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. 

**Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail
**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** at base — 1945 kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Haenyeo demonstration once daily at 14:00 (subject to bad-weather cancellation)** at the seaside cove — UNESCO Intangible 2016
**Admission updated April 2025**: Adult ₩5,000 / Youth ₩2,500 / FREE under 7 + over 65
Spring/autumn hours **05:00–19:00** (last entry 18:20)
**Closes for first Monday of each month** (occasional rest day)
timeUsed: Ascent to summit deck (0.8 km / 140 m gain) — ~30 min
Summit views + crater rim photos — ~20 min
Optional haenyeo viewing at base (14:00 show; may be canceled in bad weather) — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Main UNESCO natural-heritage highlight of the eastern route.** Seongsan Ilchulbong gives the day its strongest volcanic landscape: a dramatic tuff cone formed by underwater volcanic activity and recognized as part of Jeju's UNESCO World Natural Heritage landscape. The stop works well after the morning's village and cave visits because guests can now see the volcanic story at full scale above the sea. When timing and weather allow, the visit may also connect naturally with a haenyeo performance near the base before the route continues to the Haenyeo Museum for deeper cultural context.

sources: UNESCO WHC inscription 1264 (Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes, 2007-07-02)
UNESCO Global Geoparks Network — Jeju (2010-10 designation)
Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument designation 2000-07-19
Seongsan Ilchulbong official site (seongsan.kr) — April 2025 admission update
Jeju Haenyeo Museum — UNESCO Intangible 2016 inscription record

itineraryStops.name: Haenyeo Museum (women divers)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage — Jeju Diving Culture (2016)
itineraryStops.description:
**Jeju Haenyeo Museum (제주 해녀 박물관)** introduces the lives and culture of Jeju's women divers, whose community practice was inscribed by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2016. Located in Gujwa-eup on the east side of Jeju, the museum is close to coastal villages where haenyeo culture remains especially visible. 

Exhibitions explain diving tools, work clothes, sea-harvesting methods, village cooperatives, family transmission, songs, rituals, and the strong role women divers played in Jeju's island economy. 

The museum also helps guests understand that haenyeo are not simply a photo subject: their work reflects breath-hold diving skill, ecological knowledge, community discipline, and a long tradition of women's labor. 

This is a mostly indoor stop, useful after Seongsan Ilchulbong and helpful in rainy or windy weather.
highlights: **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (2016)** — Jeju women-diver culture
**First written record 1629** — Lee Geon's 'Description of Jeju' / 'Guchang' (recorded as 'Jamnyeo')
**3 exhibition halls** — Daily Life, Diving Practice + Categorization, Community + Future
Senior **Sanggun** dive to **10 m on 60-sec breath-hold**, ~7 hr/day × ~90 days/year
**Hagun / Junggun / Sanggun** rank system based on depth + breath-hold capacity
**Maternal lineage** — mothers train daughters from age 6–7
**Under 4,000 active haenyeo** today — mostly 70+; population diminishing
Admission **₩1,100 adult / ₩500 youth / FREE under 7 + over 65**
Hours **09:00–18:00**, **closed Tuesdays + lunar holidays**; indoor ~80% (rain-friendly)
timeUsed: Outdoor sculpture garden + replica boat — ~5 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **UNESCO intangible-heritage finale.** After the route shows Jeju's coast, old village life, lava-cave landscape, and Seongsan's volcanic scenery, the Haenyeo Museum completes the day with the human culture connected to the sea. The museum is a short drive from Seongsan and gives guests time to understand the history, tools, and community structure behind Jeju's women divers. It is also a good indoor closing stop before the return drive to Jeju City.

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Both UNESCO listings (natural + cultural) in one day
items.detail: Most Jeju eastern tours include Seongsan Ilchulbong but skip the Haenyeo Museum. This route deliberately keeps both — meaning visitors leave with both Jeju UNESCO categories: the natural heritage (Seongsan's tuff cone) and the intangible cultural heritage (haenyeo women-diver tradition, listed 2016).

items.label: Manjanggul alternate handled gracefully
items.detail: Manjanggul Lava Tube is currently closed — most tours either skip cave content entirely or substitute lazily. This route uses Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, which gives visitors a comparable lava-tube experience plus the Ilchul Land botanical garden (seasonal flower displays). When Manjanggul reopens, the operator will likely revert; check at booking.

items.label: All admissions included — value at the price tier
items.detail: Eastern Jeju includes several paid attractions such as Seongsan, Ilchul Land, and the Haenyeo Museum. Having the main admission planning handled in one route makes the day easier for guests than arranging each stop separately.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is optional-climb
items.detail: The 30-minute rim climb is steep and not for everyone. The route allocates 90 minutes total at the site, which is enough for the climb if you want it but also enough to walk the free coastal trail at the base for similar photo angles without the stairs. Visitors with mobility concerns get the UNESCO credit either way.

staticQuestions.question: Will I see live haenyeo divers?
staticQuestions.answer: The Haenyeo Museum has video footage and exhibitions about working dives. Live diving demonstrations may take place at nearby coastal areas or around Seongsan Ilchulbong depending on local schedule and weather. The tour timing is planned to improve the chance of seeing one, but a live demonstration cannot be promised.

staticQuestions.question: Can I skip the Seongsan rim climb?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — the free coastal trail at the base offers similar photo angles without the stairs. The 90-minute stop time allows either the summit walk or a calmer coastal walk, depending on mobility and preference.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Southern UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: similar local route. The Southern tour covers Hallasan trail, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon Falls, Jusangjeolli, O'sulloc — focused on natural heritage and gardens. This Eastern tour covers Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Village, Micheon Cave, Seongsan, and the Haenyeo Museum — focused on UNESCO heritage (both natural and cultural) and traditional Jeju life. Most 2-day Jeju visitors do one of each.

staticQuestions.question: What if Manjanggul reopens during my visit?
staticQuestions.answer: If Manjanggul (a more famous and longer lava tube than Micheon) reopens, the operator may swap the cave stop. Manjanggul is currently closed for ongoing safety repairs with no announced reopening date — the Micheon Cave alternate is the dependable substitute.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — lunch is at your own expense (≈ ₩15,000). The guide will recommend a local east-Jeju restaurant and assist with vegetarian, vegan, halal, or allergy needs if you advise at booking.

staticQuestions.question: Why airport Gate 1 instead of Gate 3?
staticQuestions.answer: The Eastern tour pickup uses Jeju Airport Gate 1 (1st Floor Domestic Arrivals) because the airport-to-east-coast route is more efficient via the eastern airport perimeter road. The Southern tour uses Gate 3 (3rd Floor Domestic Departures) for the southern route. This is operational; pick whichever tour is in your plan and follow the gate listed on your booking.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to eastern Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: An active day with several walking-heavy stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a full day of moving around.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

itineraryStops.name: Pickup (4 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description: **Pickup is consolidated at four central Jeju City points** to minimize morning crisscrossing: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for guests with same-day arrivals or hotels near the airport corridor; **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for guests in the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster; **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** as the westernmost pickup before heading east; and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** as the optional eastern Jeju City pickup near Tapdong waterfront. Sequence is Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → eastward Route 1132 toward Hamdeok. The minicoach is **small-group (max ~14 pax)** with English-speaking guide. **Bilingual printed itinerary card** distributed at pickup, weather brief at first stop.

Airport Gate 1 (Domestic Arrivals) for east route
Air-conditioned mini-coach
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Four pickup points cover Jeju City's main hotel + transport zones (airport, mid-city, old town, market district) without forcing guests across the city. Sequencing west→east lands the coach at Hamdeok ~09:50 — early enough to catch the morning low-tide beach panorama before peak visitor flow.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.category: Top-3 Jeju Beach — Olle Trail 19 + WWII Coastal History
itineraryStops.description: **Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (함덕 서우봉 해변)** is one of Jeju's best-known east-coast beaches, loved for its bright sand, shallow emerald water, and the small volcanic cone of **Seoubong (서우봉)** rising at the eastern end. The main beach and connected coves give guests an easy first look at Jeju's coastal scenery without a long walk, and the seaside promenade is suitable for families and seniors. In spring, the slopes around Seoubong often turn yellow with canola flowers; in clear weather, the short upper path gives a broad view over the eastern coastline. The area also has a historical layer: coastal caves and wartime remains around Seoubong are connected with Japan's late-colonial military presence on Jeju, adding context beyond the beach view. Hamdeok is also part of **Jeju Olle Trail Course 19**, so guests can experience a small piece of Jeju's walking culture before moving inland. Admission is free, and the stop works well as a relaxed photo and orientation point at the beginning of the eastern route.
highlights: Top-3 Jeju beach
Seoubong volcanic cone
Spring canola bloom on cone slopes
Easy photo stop
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Coastal opening highlight of the eastern UNESCO route.** Hamdeok gives the day an easy start with Jeju's beach scenery, a short coastal walk, and a light historical layer before the route turns inland toward Seongeup. It is naturally placed after Jeju City on the eastbound road, so guests get a meaningful first stop without unnecessary backtracking. Compared with beaches farther east or inside Jeju City, Hamdeok balances scenery, access, walking comfort, and route efficiency especially well for a one-day tour.

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO heritage)
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
itineraryStops.description: **Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju architecture, and local stories about daily life in a windy volcanic island environment. For visitors, this stop gives a grounded cultural contrast after Hamdeok's coast: guests see how Jeju people historically built homes, protected villages with stone walls, and adapted to a harsh island climate.
highlights: Lived-in heritage village
Volcanic stone walls (dol-dam)
Traditional kitchens
Korean Folklore Cultural Heritage
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Mid-morning cultural heritage highlight.** Seongeup connects the natural side of eastern Jeju with the island's human history. The visit gives guests a clear look at traditional houses, stone walls, village trees, and old administrative history before the route continues toward the lava-cave and Seongsan area. The stop is long enough for an alley walk, photos, and basic cultural explanation without making the day feel rushed.

itineraryStops.name: Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land (Manjanggul alternate)
itineraryStops.category: Lava Tube + Theme Park — Manjanggul Alternate (UNESCO-property adjacent)
itineraryStops.description: **Ilchul Land (일출랜드)** is a large nature theme park in Seongsan built around **Micheon Cave (미천굴)**, a lava-tube cave with a visitor walkway open to the public. It is not Manjanggul Cave, but it offers a practical lava-cave experience when Manjanggul is unavailable or when guests need an easier walking surface. Micheon Cave's maintained path, lighting, and controlled route make it more accessible for families, older guests, and visitors who may not be comfortable with rougher cave floors. Outside the cave, Ilchul Land includes gardens, traditional-style Jeju displays, sculptures, and rest areas, so the stop functions as both a geology stop and a weather-friendly break in the middle of the route. The cave is cooler than the outside temperature, so a light jacket can be useful even in warmer seasons.

Default rotation: **mulhoe (물회, cold raw-fish soup)** in summer (June–September), **galchi-jorim (갈치조림, braised cutlassfish)** in winter (October–February), or **heuk-doeji gui (흑돼지구이, Jeju black pig BBQ)** year-round. Black pig is a heritage breed designated **Cultural Heritage Material No. 5 (2015)**. **Vegetarian + halal-friendly options** available at one venue in the rotation — disclose at pickup. itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO Sunrise Peak)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
itineraryStops.description: **Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. **18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. **Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run **daily at 13:30 and 15:00** at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. **Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Main UNESCO natural-heritage highlight of the eastern route.** Seongsan Ilchulbong gives the day its strongest volcanic landscape: a dramatic tuff cone formed by underwater volcanic activity and recognized as part of Jeju's UNESCO World Natural Heritage landscape. The stop works well after the morning's village and cave visits because guests can now see the volcanic story at full scale above the sea. When timing and weather allow, the visit may also connect naturally with a haenyeo performance near the base before the route continues to the Haenyeo Museum for deeper cultural context.

itineraryStops.name: Haenyeo Museum (women divers)
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage — Jeju Diving Culture (2016)
itineraryStops.description: **Jeju Haenyeo Museum (제주 해녀 박물관)** introduces the lives and culture of Jeju's women divers, whose community practice was inscribed by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2016. Located in Gujwa-eup on the east side of Jeju, the museum is close to coastal villages where haenyeo culture remains especially visible. Exhibitions explain diving tools, work clothes, sea-harvesting methods, village cooperatives, family transmission, songs, rituals, and the strong role women divers played in Jeju's island economy. The museum also helps guests understand that haenyeo are not simply a photo subject: their work reflects breath-hold diving skill, ecological knowledge, community discipline, and a long tradition of women's labor. This is a mostly indoor stop, useful after Seongsan Ilchulbong and helpful in rainy or windy weather.

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **UNESCO intangible-heritage finale.** After the route shows Jeju's coast, old village life, lava-cave landscape, and Seongsan's volcanic scenery, the Haenyeo Museum completes the day with the human culture connected to the sea. The museum is a short drive from Seongsan and gives guests time to understand the history, tools, and community structure behind Jeju's women divers. It is also a good indoor closing stop before the return drive to Jeju City.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: An eastern-Jeju loop focused on Seongsan Ilchulbong, the Jeju haenyeo women-diver tradition, Seongeup Folk Village, Hamdeok Beach, and Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land. The route balances UNESCO natural heritage, intangible cultural heritage, traditional village life, and volcanic scenery while keeping admissions and travel time simple for a one-day small-group tour.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Both UNESCO listings (natural + cultural) in one day
items.detail: Most Jeju eastern tours include Seongsan Ilchulbong but skip the Haenyeo Museum. This route deliberately keeps both — meaning visitors leave with both Jeju UNESCO categories: the natural heritage (Seongsan's tuff cone) and the intangible cultural heritage (haenyeo women-diver tradition, listed 2016).

items.label: Manjanggul alternate handled gracefully
items.detail: Manjanggul Lava Tube is currently closed — most tours either skip cave content entirely or substitute lazily. This route uses Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, which gives visitors a comparable lava-tube experience plus the Ilchul Land botanical garden (seasonal flower displays). When Manjanggul reopens, the operator will likely revert; check at booking.

items.label: All admissions included — value at the price tier
items.detail: Eastern Jeju includes several paid attractions such as Seongsan, Ilchul Land, and the Haenyeo Museum. Having the main admission planning handled in one route makes the day easier for guests than arranging each stop separately.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is optional-climb
items.detail: The 30-minute rim climb is steep and not for everyone. The route allocates 90 minutes total at the site, which is enough for the climb if you want it but also enough to walk the free coastal trail at the base for similar photo angles without the stairs. Visitors with mobility concerns get the UNESCO credit either way.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Air-conditioned mini-coach, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, ALL attraction admissions (Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000, Seongeup Folk Village free, Ilchul Land/Micheon Cave ₩12,000, Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100, Hamdeok Beach free), fuel/tolls/parking. Not included: lunch (≈ ₩15,000), drinks, tips.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Sequential pickup at four points: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30), Jeju Airport Gate 1, 1F Domestic Arrivals (08:45 — note: different gate from operator's Southern tour), LOTTE City Hotel Jeju (08:55), Shilla Duty-Free Jeju Store (09:05). Choose the closest at booking. Day-before WhatsApp confirmation.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. Hamdeok Beach is flat sand. Seongeup Folk Village has dirt paths and stone walls. Micheon Cave has stairs descending into the tube and uneven flooring inside. Seongsan Ilchulbong's rim climb is 30 minutes of steep stone steps (skippable). Haenyeo Museum is flat indoor walking. Comfortable closed-toe shoes essential. Total walking ≈ 4–5 km.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup. Same-day cancellations and no-shows non-refundable. Heavy weather may shorten the Seongsan climb but does not cancel the day.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cave note (Manjanggul status)
practicalAccordionItems.content: Manjanggul Lava Tube is currently closed for safety repairs (unscheduled reopening). The Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land is the standard alternate and is included on this route. If Manjanggul reopens during your travel period, the operator may swap stops; check at booking.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Recommended for ages 10+ due to the Seongsan Ilchulbong rim climb (≈ 600 stairs, optional but the day's centerpiece) and a 9-hour pace. Younger children can join, but the climb may be too much; consider our year-round Cherry Blossom or Cruise Bus tours which skip the summit. Strollers are not recommended on Seongsan stairs or Seongeup village paths.

staticQuestions.question: Will I see live haenyeo divers?
staticQuestions.answer: The Haenyeo Museum has video footage and exhibitions about working dives. Live diving demonstrations may take place at nearby coastal areas or around Seongsan Ilchulbong depending on local schedule and weather. The tour timing is planned to improve the chance of seeing one, but a live demonstration cannot be promised.

staticQuestions.question: Can I skip the Seongsan rim climb?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — the free coastal trail at the base offers similar photo angles without the stairs. The 90-minute stop time allows either the summit walk or a calmer coastal walk, depending on mobility and preference.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Southern UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: similar local route. The Southern tour covers Hallasan trail, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon Falls, Jusangjeolli, O'sulloc — focused on natural heritage and gardens. This Eastern tour covers Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Village, Micheon Cave, Seongsan, and the Haenyeo Museum — focused on UNESCO heritage (both natural and cultural) and traditional Jeju life. Most 2-day Jeju visitors do one of each.

staticQuestions.question: What if Manjanggul reopens during my visit?
staticQuestions.answer: If Manjanggul (a more famous and longer lava tube than Micheon) reopens, the operator may swap the cave stop. Manjanggul is currently closed for ongoing safety repairs with no announced reopening date — the Micheon Cave alternate is the dependable substitute.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — lunch is at your own expense (≈ ₩15,000). The guide will recommend a local east-Jeju restaurant and assist with vegetarian, vegan, halal, or allergy needs if you advise at booking.

staticQuestions.question: Why airport Gate 1 instead of Gate 3?
staticQuestions.answer: The Eastern tour pickup uses Jeju Airport Gate 1 (1st Floor Domestic Arrivals) because the airport-to-east-coast route is more efficient via the eastern airport perimeter road. The Southern tour uses Gate 3 (3rd Floor Domestic Departures) for the southern route. This is operational; pick whichever tour is in your plan and follow the gate listed on your booking.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to eastern Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: An active day with several walking-heavy stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a full day of moving around.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 1.0 — this tour is correctly named 'eastern UNESCO loop': Seongsan = UNESCO World Heritage (Jeju Volcanic Island & Lava Tubes 2007), Haenyeo Museum = UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage (haenyeo culture 2016). Tour covers BOTH UNESCO categories. Strongest UNESCO-fit tour in catalogue.

intangible_heritage_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 1.0 — Haenyeo Museum is the dedicated UNESCO ICH interpretation center; Seongsan haenyeo demonstrations 13:30/15:00 are live UNESCO ICH performance; Seongeup Folk Village preserves Joseon-period intangible folkways. 3 of 5 attractions carry intangible-heritage value. Highest possible score.

walking_level.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 0.8→0.65 with verified totals: Hamdeok beach 0.5 km flat (sand + boardwalk), Seongeup ~1 km flat olle, Micheon Cave 365 m flat (leveled), Seongsan 0.8 km + 140 m gain (the only stair-heavy stop), Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor flat. Total ~3 km + only Seongsan has elevation. Tour 1 (east-signature) walks more (Seopjikoji 2.1 km extra). Verified moderate walking = 0.65 not 0.8.

young_kids_fit.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 1.0→0.75: Hamdeok beach + leveled Micheon Cave + indoor Haenyeo Museum + Seongeup folk village = 4 of 5 kid-friendly. ONLY Seongsan's 300-500 steps challenge under-7s. Mechanical 1.0 was overstated; verified 0.75 is honest.

stroller_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.50: Hamdeok flat + Micheon Cave LEVELED FLOOR (verified — vs Manjanggul's uneven natural floor) + Haenyeo Museum 100% accessible + Seongeup main alleys + ONLY Seongsan stairs unsuitable. 4/5 stroller-friendly = 0.50 not the previous 0.4. indoor_ratio.reasoning: MAJOR CORRECTION: Verified breakdown — Hamdeok 100% outdoor, Seongeup ~30% indoor (visiting hanok interiors), Micheon Cave 100% indoor, Seongsan 5% indoor (visitor center only), Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor. Time-weighted indoor ratio ~40%, NOT the previous 10%. Correcting a documentation error.

rain_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.75: With Micheon Cave (indoor), Haenyeo Museum (indoor), and Seongeup hanok interiors as rain refuges, this tour holds up MUCH better in rain than the mechanical 0.6 implied. Hamdeok and Seongsan suffer in rain but the rest is fine. Verified.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 0.85: Seongsan = universally iconic (triple UNESCO crown). Hamdeok = Top 3 Jeju beach. Seongeup + Haenyeo Museum = nationally significant culture (UNESCO ICH for Haenyeo). Micheon Cave = secondary lava tube. 0.85 — strong but not all 5 universally iconic.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified fees: Hamdeok free + Seongeup free + Micheon ₩12,000 + Seongsan ₩2,000 + Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100 = ~₩15,100/adult. Reasonable but Ilchul Land at ₩12,000 is the big driver. 0.85 calibrated.

senior_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.55: With Hamdeok beach walk (flat), Micheon leveled cave, Seongeup flat alleys, Haenyeo Museum accessible — 4 of 5 stops fully senior-accessible. Only Seongsan summit excludes seniors with mobility issues (alternative: stay at base for haenyeo demo). Verified ≥0.55, much better than 0.4.

senior_general_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.55: Same logic — 4/5 stops accessible to general senior population (alternative for Seongsan = stay at base for haenyeo demonstration). 0.55 calibrated.

beach_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.85: Hamdeok IS the dedicated beach stop — Top 3 Jeju beach (verified VisitKorea ranking). 0.6 understates this; 0.85 reflects beach as a primary tour theme.
